criteria.planned_check: Static: ReadSegment's function body is exactly `records, _, err := readSegmentFrom(path, 0); return records, err` (verified by reading the diff).

criteria.text: Eliminates the duplicated CRC-check/parsing logic between writer.go and recovery.go.
criteria.planned_check: Static: after the change, only readSegmentFrom performs the os.ReadFile + parseSegmentRecords sequence; ReadSegment no longer has its own copy of that logic. Confirmed by grep for os.ReadFile in writer.go's ReadSegment body (absent post-change).

criteria.text: Both sharing a single extracted parsing helper.
criteria.planned_check: Static: parseSegmentRecords remains the sole low-level parser, now reached transitively by ReadSegment only through readSegmentFrom (not called directly by ReadSegment anymore); still called directly by repairTornTail (unrelated, unchanged) and recovery_test.go's white-box test (unchanged).

criteria.text: No behavior change (existing error semantics, torn-tail vs CRC-corruption handling, and public signature all preserved).
criteria.planned_check: go test ./engine/wal/... -race -count=1 green with zero test-file modifications; go test ./engine/catalog/... ./engine/graph/... ./engine/mvcc/... green (all call wal.ReadSegment directly).
Run go test ./wal/... -race -count=1 from the engine module root; must exit 0.

criteria.text: Doc-comment conventions maintained (per repo's Go doc-comment standard, sibling commits bcfb1d8/cc16c25/9774bee).
